reasoning: The critical driver here is an ex-dividend date confirmed for Tuesday, March 31, 2026, which falls squarely between the forecast date (weekend of Mar 29, so Mar 27 close) and the resolution date (weekend of Apr 5. Since Good Friday is April 3, the final close is Thurs Apr 2). The upcoming dividend is approximately $0.33 per quarter. Given BEN's current stock price of roughly $23.67, the ex-dividend will force a mechanical drop of ~1.4% at the open on March 31. Because there are only 4 trading days in this period (March 30 to April 2), the variance is lower than a standard week, meaning it is mathematically more difficult for BEN's natural movements to overcome this fixed -1.4% handicap. Assuming a 4-day volatility of about 3% and a slight positive market drift, the probability of the stock closing higher strictly due to outperforming the dividend drag is straightforwardly calculated using a Z-score (~0.42 sigma hurdle), which yields roughly a 33-34% chance.
